lines.text: We're {{ELAPSED}} in and the West Side is waking up. Nakoma, Hilldale, Monroe Street corridor — these are supposed to be the quiet zones. These are supposed to be where the money buys you peace.
Monroe Street's seeing a spike. Not massive, but persistent. Pattern suggests coordinated movement from the Beltline west. Vehicle-based, likely.
Temperature's holding at 26 degrees. Wind backed off an hour ago. Perfect conditions for someone who wants to move through the city unimpeded.

Perfect conditions. That's one way to put it. I used to have a house on Monroe Street. Had. Past tense. Sold it in '66 because I couldn't sleep on Purge Night knowing what was happening outside my window.
I could have. But barricading is a choice, Holden. It's an admission. And I wasn't ready to admit what I'd become.
Incoming text says there's a fire on Regent Street. Small structure. Spreading north. Wind's going to push it toward the Nakoma residential cluster in about forty minutes if nobody's dealing with it.
Nobody's dealing with it. It's 01:00. We're in the Purge.
Call it what it is, Holden. Don't call it 'the Purge' like it's natural. Like it's weather.
It is weather now. It's part of the season. Purge Night. Spring thaw. Winter freeze. Purge Night.

tickerItems.text: West Side cumulative: 18. Monroe Street sector: 6 (up 4 since 00:30).
Structure fire reported Regent Street / Monroe intersection. Spreading north toward Nakoma residential. Wind favorable for containment (no containment possible).
Hilldale commercial corridor: PurgeShield panels breached at 3 storefronts. Looting confirmed. No body count yet.
Text from Nakoma resident: 'We can see the fire from our house. It's beautiful and I hate that I think that.' — Quinn has no response.
